summary: Parallel review: FAIL (1 critical, 14 warning, 9 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 bin/lib/run.mjs:1507 — Fix-loop behavior validated only by source-text regex (test:473–500); no behavioral test exercises the loop with a mocked `runSimplifyPass` returning persistent criticals; escalation trigger and finalize-block are untested end-to-end — extract loop body to a unit-testable helper or add behavioral integration tests

🟡 bin/lib/run.mjs:1595 — `phaseOrder` hardcodes `["brainstorm", "build", "review"]`; `"simplify"` absent despite `setUsageContext("simplify", null)` at line 1506 — simplify-pass dispatch cost invisible in per-phase console breakdown and progress log (carried through 5+ reviews without fix)

The primary concern is that the new fix-loop behavior (task-3's core contribution) is verified only by source-text assertions rather than behavioral tests. The `runSimplifyPass` unit in `simplify-pass.mjs` has excellent injectable-dependency coverage, but the loop logic that calls it 1–3 times and sets `escalated` has no equivalent. This is a test coverage gap, not a logic bug — but it means loop-level regressions would go undetected. Filed to backlog as 🟡.

🟡 `bin/lib/simplify-pass.mjs:147` — dispatch-fail return missing `findings` field; handshake claims "return findings in all post-dispatch result objects" but this path (`!result.ok`) returns without it; add `findings: { critical: 0, warning: 0, suggestion: 0 }` and assert it in `test/simplify-pass.test.mjs:311`

🟡 `test/simplify-pass.test.mjs:466` — 500-char source-inspection window to find the `completed > 0 && blocked === 0` guard is marginally safe (~460 chars currently used); inserting any log between guard and call would silently break the test — increase window to 600 or anchor on the guard string directly

🟡 bin/lib/run.mjs:1584 — Escalated case absent from completion report summary; the summary block shows `filesChanged > 0` or `reverted` but has no branch for `escalated` — operator sees only the inline log, not the final box. Add `else if (simplifyResult.escalated)` entry.

🟡 bin/lib/run.mjs:1508 — Fix loop re-dispatches agent on already-reverted code: when a round returns `reverted: true` with `findings.critical > 0`, the next iteration sees the same unchanged code, produces the same criticals, gate fails again, and reverts again. All 2 fix rounds are consumed with no possibility of progress. Bounded, but add early-exit when `reverted && critical > 0`.

All three core requirements are met with direct evidence: critical findings block finalize (`run.mjs:1543–1547`), the fix loop runs up to `MAX_SIMPLIFY_FIX_ROUNDS=2` additional rounds (`run.mjs:1507–1517`), and escalation fires with console + progress.md output when rounds are exhausted. `parseSimplifyFindings` is fully unit-tested. No red findings. Two 🟡 items filed to backlog; neither blocks merge.

🟡 `test/simplify-pass.test.mjs:473` — All five fix-loop tests are source-text assertions; missing behavioral coverage: loop re-dispatches on criticals, stops when cleared, sets `escalated: true` after rounds exhausted, finalize skipped at runtime — source assertions pass even with a logically broken loop; same gap pattern flagged 🟡 across prior review cycles for the guard-condition assertion

The implementation is correct by direct code inspection. `parseSimplifyFindings` has complete behavioral coverage (7 tests). The findings return-value tests are behavioral across four paths. The critical gap is that all five tests covering the fix loop — the core new feature of this task — are source-text assertions that verify code structure but cannot catch runtime behavioral regressions. The logic is sound; the regression protection is weak. One new 🟡 filed to backlog. One 🔵 suggestion. Escalated-in-summary gap corroborates the PM finding already in the eval. No blockers.

🟡 bin/lib/simplify-pass.mjs:82 — File paths from `git diff --name-only` embedded in agent prompt without sanitization; the fix loop (run.mjs:1507–1518) re-dispatches up to 3 times with the same unsanitized brief, each with `--permission-mode bypassPermissions`; strip control characters before embedding e.g. `f.replace(/[\x00-\x1f\x7f]/g, "").trim()` — risk amplified by the new fix loop; carried from prior security passes

🟡 bin/lib/simplify-pass.mjs:47 — `getChangedFiles` is a public export accepting caller-supplied `base` without SHA format validation before shell interpolation; add `/^[0-9a-f]{7,40}$/i` guard to harden the exported API surface (carried from task-2 reviews)
No 🔴 critical findings across all four veto categories. Two 🟡 warnings:
🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:1510` — `simplifyResult.findings.critical` without optional chaining; safe by loop invariant but fragile if invariant weakens — use `simplifyResult.findings?.critical ?? 0`

🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:1506` + `1595` — `setUsageContext("simplify", null)` records simplify-phase token usage but `phaseOrder` excludes `"simplify"`, so those costs are silently dropped from the completion report
